title: pollinia undata
intro: Pollinia undata, also known as the "sensitive plant" or "touch-me-not," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pollinia undata include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
